[Insights](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/pulse) # Add a setting to disable the sidebar transparency / blur \#3397 Closed [MoltenMonster](https://github.com/MoltenMonster) started this conversation in [Ideas](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/categories/ideas) [Add a setting to disable the sidebar transparency / blur](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/3397#top)#3397 [![@MoltenMonster](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42647030?s=40&v=4)\\ MoltenMonster](https://github.com/MoltenMonster) on Dec 5, 2024Dec 5, 2024· 6 comments · 7 replies [Return to top](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/3397#top) Discussion options # {{title}} Quote reply edited # {{editor}}'s edit {{actor}} deleted this content . # {{editor}}'s edit ## [![](https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/42647030?s=64&v=4)\ MoltenMonster](https://github.com/MoltenMonster) [on Dec 5, 2024Dec 5, 2024](https://github.com/zen-browser/desktop/discussions/3397\#discussion-7614687) Original comment in English - Translate to English | | | --- | | Its an opinionated aesthetic choice with some users prefer to be turned off, especially to fully see color themes. For now here's a userChrome CSS workaround:
```
#zen-main-app-wrapper, #zen-appcontent-wrapper, #zen-sidebar-splitter {
appearance: unset !important;
}
``` | BetaWas this translation helpful? It's really annoying when browser just decide to change color on its own. I had a colorful background, but I don't want browser to change based on that. | BetaWas this translation helpful? ![image](https://private-user-images.githubusercontent.com/572141/427921178-eab018f9-01e8-4eb1-bec2-fcceb23b705c.png?jwt=eyJ0eXAiOiJKV1QiLCJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiJ9.eyJpc3MiOiJnaXRodWIuY29tIiwiYXVkIjoicmF3LmdpdGh1YnVzZXJjb250ZW50LmNvbSIsImtleSI6ImtleTUiLCJleHAiOjE3ODIzMTk5NjcsIm5iZiI6MTc4MjMxOTY2NywicGF0aCI6Ii81NzIxNDEvNDI3OTIxMTc4LWVhYjAxOGY5LTAxZTgtNGViMS1iZWMyLWZjY2ViMjNiNzA1Yy5wbmc_.vo-lNBn4XnxcBz2FllUnc3Ym9O5laD8Uuc01xvpV4_Q)
It's just bad.
This is the first time I've tried Zen and I like it so far. There are a few interface habits I will have to get use to, but this sidebar transparency is just wrong.
Great work otherwise, people. Fantastic browser. | BetaWas this translation helpful? metadata.description: Add a setting to disable the sidebar transparency / blur
metadata.og:description: Its an opinionated aesthetic choice with some users prefer to be turned off, especially to fully see color themes. For now here's a userChrome CSS workaround: #zen-main-app-wrapper, #zen-appcontent...
